what_it_does: This file implements a registry-driven embedding client that calls a served embeddings stack over HTTP. It exposes functions to embed single texts, queries, exchanges, document chunks, and batches of exchanges, and it maps lens metadata to a vec0 storage specification. All model, dimension, endpoint, metric, and prefix values are read from a LensEntry, and errors throw rather than falling back.
observations: A single lens entry 'pplx-ctx-4b-docs' is registered with dim 2560, int8 encoding, cosine metric, and an empty query prefix.
The active lens name and endpoint URL are overridable via EMBED_LENS and EMBED_URL environment variables.
Document and query embedding paths are routed through the same lens entry to keep the vector spaces aligned.
The file distinguishes flat single-chunk mode ({input:[...]}) from documents-mode ({documents:[[...]]}) and explicitly routes batch exchange embedding through flat mode.
A deterministic MAX_EMBED_CHARS cap (24000) truncates over-long exchange text from the head and appends a '[…embed-capped]' marker.
BGE_QUERY_PREFIX is exported for historical interface stability but is now derived from the active lens's queryPrefix, which is empty for pplx.
lensVecSpec validates encoding and metric mappings and throws for unsupported combinations such as inner_product.
Comments extensively cite Guide sections, landmines, and seam identifiers, framing the code as part of a larger migration/schema effort.
standouts: Registry-is-truth design: no model, dim, URL, metric, or prefix literals appear in the call bodies.
Fail-loud behavior on missing lens, transport error, HTTP error, wrong count, or wrong dimension — no zero-vector or in-process fallback.
Explicit guard routing batch exchange embedding through flat mode rather than documents-mode to prevent unrelated exchanges from bleeding context.
conventions: Read-only Map registry keyed by lens name.
Default parameter lens = getLens() repeated across exported functions.
TypeScript interfaces for lens, storage spec, and response shapes.
Async fetch with try/catch and explicit error messages.
Deterministic truncation with an auditable marker for oversized inputs.
concerns: BGE_QUERY_PREFIX is a misleading name now that the active lens is pplx-ctx-4b-docs and the prefix is empty.
The module-level const BGE_QUERY_PREFIX is initialized at load time via getLens(), so a later EMBED_LENS change will not update it.
MAX_EMBED_CHARS is a character count, not a token count, so it may not precisely match the server's tokenizer cap.
postEmbeddings uses a bare fetch with no timeout, abort signal, or retry logic.
orderedVectors sorts by index only if every item has an index; a mixed response could silently fall back to array order.
LensEntry.metric allows 'inner_product' but lensVecSpec rejects it, creating a type/runtime mismatch for future lenses.
notes: A heavily documented, fail-loud embedding client that centralizes all embedding-space metadata in a registry and delegates actual vector generation to a remote served stack.
questions: Where is the served embedding stack implemented and how is the EMBED_URL allowlist in .mcp.json maintained?
How does the storage layer consume LensVecSpec to create and address vec_<space> tables?
How will multiple lenses be selected at query time once the registry grows beyond one entry?
What is the exact behavior of the documents-mode response index field when chunks are late-chunked?
How is EMBEDDING_VERSION bumped and coordinated with the vec0 rebuild mentioned in the comments?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose—replacing the in-process ONNX embedder with a registry-driven, served-stack, fail-loud lens—matches the implementation.
apparent_themes: same-space golden rule for index and query
intention_signals: It is trying to make the choice of embedding space fully data-driven and configurable, isolate the HTTP transport to one place, prevent silent wrong-dimension or wrong-space vectors, and guarantee that single and batch embedding paths produce identical vectors for the same input.
novelty: The file is unusually documentation-dense and introduces a lens registry abstraction that separates embedding-space metadata from transport and storage mapping, with explicit guards against accidentally mixing flat and documents-mode calls.
contribution: It contributes a centralized, registry-driven embedding client that exposes stable single, query, exchange, chunk, and batch embedding interfaces, plus a lens-to-vec0 mapping, so downstream callers do not hardcode model, dimension, endpoint, or metric.
summary_for_embedding: A TypeScript embedding client that reads a lens registry to call a served embeddings endpoint, exposing single, query, exchange, chunk, and batch embedding functions while enforcing same-space indexing and fail-loud error handling.
intention_for_embedding: To make the embedding space configurable via a registry, eliminate in-process model loading, and ensure document and query vectors are produced through identical lens entries so search spaces stay aligned.

symbols.does: Declares the registry descriptor for one embedding space.
symbols.description: Interface capturing space name, model, dimension, metric, endpoint URL, encoding, and optional asymmetric query prefix. It is the registry's unit of truth so that embedding call bodies never hardcode model or dimension values.

symbols.does: Returns the name of the currently selected lens.
symbols.description: Reads process.env.EMBED_LENS, defaulting to 'pplx-ctx-4b-docs', so the active embedding space can be switched by configuration without editing the registry.

symbols.does: Resolves a registered lens entry by name.
symbols.description: Looks up the named lens in LENS_REGISTRY and throws a descriptive error if missing; defaulting to the active lens name makes it the single call site that selects the embedding space.

symbols.does: Storage descriptor for a vec0 table derived from a lens.
symbols.description: Interface carrying space, dimension, element type, and distance metric in the form the storage layer expects; it bridges lens encoding and metric values to vec0 column declarations.

symbols.does: Converts a LensEntry into a LensVecSpec for vec0 storage.
symbols.description: Maps lens.encoding to 'int8' or 'float' and lens.metric to 'cosine' or 'L2', throwing for unsupported values so a misconfigured lens cannot silently create the wrong table type.

symbols.does: Validates the active lens at boot time.
symbols.description: No-op warmup that calls getLens() to surface a missing or misconfigured active lens early, preserving the historical async initialization signature.

symbols.does: Embeds a single text using the active lens in flat mode.
symbols.description: Routes one string through embedFlat against the active lens and returns the single resulting vector; it is the symmetric document/passage embedding entry point.

symbols.does: Prepends the lens's retrieval prefix to a query if configured.
symbols.description: Returns the query unchanged when the prefix is empty; otherwise idempotently prepends the lens.queryPrefix, supporting asymmetric retrieval models while keeping the function registry-driven.

symbols.does: Embeds a query in a specific lens/axis after applying its query prefix.
symbols.description: Combines withQueryPrefix and embedFlat so a query vector is produced in the same space as documents for the chosen lens, enforcing the same-space golden rule for axis-selective search.

symbols.does: Embeds a query using the active lens and its query prefix.
symbols.description: Delegates to generateQueryEmbeddingForLens with the active lens, ensuring routine query embedding shares the exact lens entry used by document embedding.

symbols.does: Embeds a combined exchange document from user, assistant, and tool names.
symbols.description: Builds the canonical exchange text via exchangeDocText and embeds it unmodified through generateEmbedding, so an exchange is represented by one searchable vector in the active lens space.

symbols.does: Embeds multiple chunks of one document in documents-mode.
symbols.description: Sends chunks as a single documents-mode request to the served stack and returns one vector per chunk, preserving order via the response index; intended for contextual chunking, not for unrelated exchanges.

symbols.does: Builds the canonical text representation of an exchange and caps its length.
symbols.description: Concatenates user message, assistant message, and optional tool names, then deterministically truncates at MAX_EMBED_CHARS from the head and appends a marker when capped, ensuring re-embed stability and protecting the server from oversized inputs.

symbols.does: Batch-embeds multiple independent exchanges in one flat-mode call.
symbols.description: Maps each item through exchangeDocText and calls embedFlat, deliberately avoiding documents-mode so unrelated exchanges do not share context; produces vectors identical to the single-exchange path for the same inputs.

symbols.does: Type for the served stack's JSON response shape.
symbols.description: Declares an optional data array whose items contain an embedding vector and an optional index, matching the OpenAI-style embeddings contract consumed by orderedVectors.

symbols.does: Sends a flat single-chunk embedding request for one or more texts.
symbols.description: POSTs {input: texts} to the lens URL and returns orderedVectors validated against the lens dimension; used for single texts, queries, and batched independent exchanges.

symbols.does: Sends a documents-mode embedding request for chunks of one document.
symbols.description: POSTs {documents: [chunks]} to the lens URL and returns orderedVectors; this is the late-chunking path that yields one embedding per chunk within a single document context.

symbols.does: Performs the HTTP POST to the lens embeddings endpoint.
symbols.description: Uses fetch with JSON body and Content-Type header, throwing explicit errors on network failure or non-OK HTTP status; it is the only function aware of the HTTP transport shape and emits fail-loud messages with no fallback.

symbols.does: Extracts and validates vectors from an OpenAI-shaped response in input order.
symbols.description: Checks the response count, sorts by index when every item has one, and verifies each vector length equals lens.dim; throws on mismatches to prevent wrong-dimension vectors from poisoning the embedding space.
